title: S&P 500 forecast: What now for US stock markets?
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The S&P 500 is expected to rally 12% this year according to Goldman Sachs, while short-term forecasts suggest a 2.10% rise in the next three months. The current
excerpt: The S&P 500 is expected to rally 12% this year according to Goldman Sachs, while short-term forecasts suggest a 2.10% rise in the next three months. The current trend is positive but challenging to justify bullishly.

content.introduction: The S&P 500 is expected to rally 12% this year according to Goldman Sachs, while short-term forecasts suggest a 2.10% rise in the next three months. The current trend is positive but challenging to justify bullishly.
body: While the current trend is positive, it is becoming increasingly difficult to justify holding a bullish S&P 500 forecast.
Goldman Sachs Research predicts US stocks will rally again in 2026 amid solid economic growth and continued easing from the Fed.
Given the current short-term trend, the index is expected to rise 2.10% during the next 3 months and, with a 90% probability hold a price between 6,533.18
